metadata_information.title: The need for multiple types of information to inform climate change assessment
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: The need for multiple types of information to inform climate change assessment

document_description.abstract: Information on ecosystem characteristics as well as economic statistics is needed to more fully inform decision makers on the impacts of climate change on human well-being. Climate change risks involve potentially large and irreversible as well as highly uncertain impacts that need to be evaluated with information that complements cost-benefit analysis. Information on the irreversibility of impacts also is relevant for evaluating implications for intergenerational equity. In addition, climate change is subject to a large degree of Knightian uncertainty, making it useful to understand how individuals perceive and evaluate climate change risks.
